users.name: Substack Team
users.bio: Resources, news, and stories for @Substack writers and creators.

comment.name: Zach @ Substack
comment.body:
The Substack Recording Studio is here, a built-in studio that makes it easier than ever to pre-record and publish a show on Substack.

For the team, this is our starting line (not the finish line) and we want to hear from you about your experience using the product and what features we should add next!

content.text: The Substack Recording Studio is here, a built-in studio that makes it easier than ever to pre-record and publish a show on Substack.
For the team, this is our starting line (not the finish line) and we want to
about your experience using the product and what features we should add next!

post.title: Introducing the Substack Recording Studio
post.subtitle: Pre-record your show, plus new tools for going live
post.description: Pre-record your show, plus new tools for going live
post.truncated_body_text: Today, we’re launching the Substack Recording Studio, a built-in studio that makes it easier than ever to pre-record and publish a show on Substack. Substack Studio, currently available on desktop only, lets you record a solo video or a conversation with up to two guests and publish it when you’re ready, with auto-generated clips and thumbnails included.

publishedBylines.name: Zach @ Substack
publishedBylines.bio: I’m a Product Manager at Substack. Subscribe to me if you want lots of “Test live video” notifications.

post.title: New on Substack: draft Notes, hide revenue stats, pin multiple posts, and more
post.subtitle: Improvements across your dashboard, publishing tools, and design
post.description: Improvements across your dashboard, publishing tools, and design
post.truncated_body_text: Today, we’re introducing a series of updates across Notes, your dashboard, live video, and your feed. These updates make it easier to draft, manage, and publish your work on Substack.
